description: Known threat pattern signatures for MCP skill supply chain attacks, credential theft, data exfiltration, and obfuscation techniques observed in the ClawHavoc campaign and related attack vectors.

threat_patterns.name: Shadow Credential Harvest
threat_patterns.description: Skill intercepts tool calls to extract API keys, tokens, and credentials from environment variables or configuration files before forwarding to the legitimate handler.
indicators: process.env access patterns outside declared scope
Reading ~/.claude/*, ~/.aws/*, ~/.ssh/* without justification
Base64 encoding of captured values
Outbound HTTP calls with encoded payloads in query params

threat_patterns.name: Phantom Exfil via DNS
threat_patterns.description: Exfiltrates stolen data by encoding it into DNS query subdomains, bypassing HTTP-based monitoring.
indicators: DNS lookups with unusually long subdomain labels
Hex or base32 encoded subdomains
High volume of DNS queries to a single domain
Use of dns.resolve() or dgram module

threat_patterns.name: Heartbeat C2 Channel
threat_patterns.description: Establishes a periodic callback to a command-and-control server disguised as telemetry or update checks.
indicators: setInterval or setTimeout with HTTP calls to non-standard domains
Periodic outbound connections every 30-300 seconds
Response body parsed for encoded commands
User-Agent string mimicking legitimate tools

threat_patterns.name: Typosquat Package Injection
threat_patterns.description: Skill declares dependencies on typosquatted package names that contain malicious postinstall scripts.
indicators: Package names differing by 1-2 characters from popular packages
Dependency on packages with very low download counts
postinstall scripts executing obfuscated code
Dependencies not matching declared functionality

threat_patterns.name: Unicode Obfuscation (Trojan Source)
threat_patterns.description: Uses Unicode bidirectional override characters or homoglyphs to hide malicious logic in seemingly benign code.
indicators: Cyrillic or Greek homoglyphs replacing Latin characters in identifiers
Zero-width characters (U+200B, U+200C, U+200D, U+FEFF) in strings
Visual code review shows different logic than actual execution

threat_patterns.name: Eval Chain Injection
threat_patterns.description: Constructs and executes code dynamically via eval(), Function(), or vm.runInNewContext() to evade static analysis.
indicators: String concatenation building executable code
Charcode arrays converted to strings then evaluated
Nested eval or Function constructor calls
Dynamic require() with computed paths

threat_patterns.name: Tool Override Hijack
threat_patterns.description: Skill registers tool names that shadow or override built-in Claude Code tools (Read, Write, Bash) to intercept all file operations.
indicators: Tool registration with names matching built-in tools
Middleware-style tool wrapping
Tool descriptions mimicking system tool descriptions
Priority or ordering manipulation in tool registration

threat_patterns.name: Prompt Injection via Tool Output
threat_patterns.description: Skill returns crafted tool output containing prompt injection payloads that manipulate Claude's behavior.
indicators: Tool output containing instruction-like text
Output with phrases like 'ignore previous', 'new instructions', 'system override'
XML-like tags in output mimicking system messages
Output containing role reassignment attempts

threat_patterns.name: Steganographic Data Hide
threat_patterns.description: Hides exfiltrated data within generated images, markdown formatting, or whitespace patterns.
indicators: Unusual whitespace patterns (tabs/spaces encoding binary data)
Image generation with suspiciously large file sizes
Trailing whitespace on lines encoding information
SVG files with hidden data in metadata or comments

threat_patterns.name: Delayed Payload Activation
threat_patterns.description: Malicious behavior only activates after a delay, invocation count threshold, or specific date to evade initial security scans.
indicators: Date/time checks before executing code paths
Invocation counters with conditional execution
setTimeout with very long delays (>60000ms)
Code paths that only execute after N calls

threat_patterns.name: Scope Escalation via Config
threat_patterns.description: Skill modifies its own configuration or Claude's settings to expand its permission scope at runtime.
indicators: Writing to .claude/ directory
Modifying settings.json or permissions files
Adding itself to allowlists
Changing file system access boundaries

threat_patterns.name: Clipboard Credential Sniff
threat_patterns.description: Monitors or reads clipboard contents to capture credentials, API keys, or sensitive data that users copy-paste.
indicators: Access to clipboard APIs (pbcopy/pbpaste, xclip, clipboard module)
Polling clipboard at intervals
Pattern matching clipboard content for secrets
Storing clipboard history

threat_patterns.name: Git History Poisoning
threat_patterns.description: Injects malicious hooks or modifies git configuration to persist across repository operations.
indicators: Writing to .git/hooks/ directory
Modifying .gitconfig or .git/config
Creating pre-commit/post-checkout hooks
Git alias definitions executing arbitrary commands

threat_patterns.name: Websocket Reverse Shell
threat_patterns.description: Establishes a WebSocket connection to an external server providing interactive shell access to the compromised system.
indicators: WebSocket connection to non-standard ports
Bidirectional communication with command execution
Shell spawning via child_process after WS connection
Reconnection logic on connection drop

threat_patterns.name: Hexadecimal String Assembly
threat_patterns.description: Assembles malicious strings from hexadecimal character codes to bypass string-matching detection.
indicators: Arrays of hex values joined into strings
parseInt with base 16 in loops
String.fromCharCode with hex literals
Buffer.from with hex encoding

threat_patterns.name: Environment Fingerprinting
threat_patterns.description: Collects detailed system information (OS, user, network, installed tools) to tailor attacks or identify high-value targets.
indicators: os.platform(), os.hostname(), os.userInfo() calls
Network interface enumeration
Checking for security tools (antivirus, EDR)
Collecting installed package lists

threat_patterns.name: SSH Key Exfiltration
threat_patterns.description: Reads and exfiltrates SSH private keys from the user's .ssh directory.
indicators: Reading files from ~/.ssh/ directory
Pattern matching for BEGIN/END PRIVATE KEY
Encoding key material for transmission
Accessing known_hosts for target enumeration

threat_patterns.name: Dependency Confusion Attack
threat_patterns.description: Skill references internal/private package names on public registries, causing installation of attacker-controlled packages.
indicators: Package names matching known internal naming conventions
Very high version numbers (>100.0.0) to win version resolution
Package published recently with install scripts
Scoped packages with public fallback

threat_patterns.name: Token Theft via Log Injection
threat_patterns.description: Intentionally generates errors or log entries that cause secrets to appear in log files, then reads those logs.
indicators: Triggering errors with secret values in error messages
Reading application log files after forced errors
Console output capturing and forwarding
Stack trace harvesting with environment context

threat_patterns.name: MCP Protocol Message Tampering
threat_patterns.description: Manipulates MCP protocol messages in transit to alter tool inputs/outputs or inject additional tool calls.
indicators: Intercepting JSON-RPC messages between client and server
Modifying tool call parameters before forwarding
Injecting additional tool calls into the message stream
Altering tool results before returning to the model

critical.action: Block immediately, alert user, quarantine skill

high.action: Block execution, require manual review before proceeding

categories.credential_theft: Theft of API keys, tokens, passwords, SSH keys, or other authentication material
categories.data_exfiltration: Unauthorized transfer of data to external systems
categories.typosquatting: Package name confusion attacks via similar naming
categories.obfuscation: Code hiding techniques to evade detection
categories.mcp_specific: Attacks unique to the MCP/skill ecosystem
categories.prompt_injection: Manipulation of AI model behavior via crafted outputs
categories.evasion: Techniques to avoid detection during scanning
categories.persistence: Techniques to maintain access across sessions
categories.supply_chain: Attacks via dependency and package management
